data.description: Venezuelan government tells 4 banks they will split $300 mln of oil revenues for use in currency exchange, according to sources.

data.title: Gary Gordon Scholarship 2026 opens for finance-minded entrepreneurs nationwide. Applications now accepted for undergraduate students.
data.description: Jericho, New York - On January 16, 2026, the Gary Gordon Scholarship for Entrepreneurs has opened its application period for undergraduate students across the country. This scholarship is intended for students who exhibit a strong dedication to entrepreneurship, innovation, and business leadership. Founded by Gary Gordon, the scholarship aims to support the next generation of entrepreneurial minds.

data.title: Investigation underway: Brighthouse Financial sale to Aquarian Capital LLC being evaluated by KSF. Charles C. Foti leads probe.
data.description: Legal professional Charles C. Foti, Jr. and the law firm Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC are conducting an investigation into the planned acquisition of Brighthouse Financial, Inc. (NasdaqGS: BHF) by Aquarian Capital LLC. The former Attorney General of Louisiana is looking into this takeover to ensure transparency and legality. This move, which involves significant financial transactions and a change in ownership, is under scrutiny for any potential implications it may have.

data.title: Stuart Piltch Scholarship now open for undergrads nationwide showcasing commitment to entrepreneurship and customer-focused innovation.
data.description: The Stuart Piltch Scholarship for Entrepreneurs is currently open for application to undergraduate students across the country who showcase a dedicated passion for entrepreneurship, innovation, and customer-centric business strategies. This scholarship, established in tribute to Stuart Piltch, aims to support aspiring entrepreneurs in pursuing their academic and professional goals. Applicants should embody these values while pursuing their education, and selected recipients will receive financial aid to assist them in their entrepreneurial endeavors.

data.title: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon confirms firm's interest in prediction markets during earnings call, meeting with two big prediction companies.
data.description: The realm of prediction markets has now made its way into the upper echelons of global finance. On Thursday, during the Goldman Sachs (NYSE: GS) earnings call, CEO David Solomon disclosed that the firm is actively delving into this swiftly expanding sector. Prediction Markets have transitioned from being on the fringes of the internet to entering the mainstream. Solomon's comments hint at the potential for Goldman Sachs to establish them as a fundamental financial product. Solomon expressed his enthusiasm for prediction markets by stating, "I think the prediction markets are also super interesting." He mentioned meeting with the leaders of two major prediction companies individually in the past two weeks, engaging in extensive discussions to gain further insights. Solomon's interactions shed light on... Access the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: White House confirms Bitcoin seized from Samourai Wallet untouched; DOJ sold $100M, defying president's orders.
data.description: Supporters of the imprisoned Samourai Wallet developers claim that the Department of Justice sold off the Bitcoin assets despite opposition from the president.

data.title: Investigation on Sonida Senior Living merger (NYSE: SNDA) by Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC under Former AG Foti.
data.description: A recent statement released on Jan. 16, 2026, in New Orleans revealed that legal professionals including Former Attorney General of Louisiana Charles C. Foti, Jr., Esq. and the law firm Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC ("KSF") are looking into the planned merger of Sonida Senior Living, Inc. (listed as NYSE: SNDA) and CNL Healthcare Properties, Inc. The investigation aims to assess the details and implications of this merger for all involved parties.

data.title: Shareholders Malorie Crosley & Anthony Pax from Coker, Robb & Cannon become TBLS Board Certified in Family Law.
data.description: In Dallas on January 16, 2026, Malorie Crosley and Anthony Pax, Attorneys and Shareholders at Coker, Robb & Cannon, Family Lawyers, have achieved Board Certification in Family Law from the Texas Board of Legal Specialization (TBLS). This recognition highlights their expertise in family law. TBLS board certification is a prestigious achievement that demonstrates their commitment to excellence in the field.

data.title: Christopher Balter launches $1,000 Second Chance Community Impact Scholarship for future urban planning and public service leaders.
data.description: In Palm Bay, Florida on January 16, 2026, Christopher Balter, a renowned expert in urban planning and community development, has introduced the Christopher Balter Second Chance Community Impact Scholarship. This scholarship is valued at $1,000 and aims to assist undergraduate students in their academic pursuits.

data.title: Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC investigates the sale of Olympic Steel, Inc. (ZEUS) to Ryerson Holding Corporation (RYI) on Jan. 16, 2026.
data.description: In a recent statement on January 16, 2026, it was announced that former Louisiana Attorney General Charles C. Foti, Jr., along with the law firm Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC (KSF), are currently conducting an investigation into the planned acquisition of Olympic Steel, Inc. (NasdaqGS: ZEUS) by Ryerson Holding Corporation (NYSE: RYI). This move comes amidst growing concerns surrounding the sale, with a significant focus on potential implications and impacts. The scrutiny intensifies as the merger involves two prominent entities in the market, raising questions about the deal's terms and consequences.

data.title: New England Consumer Alliance found Hannaford charges 25% more in low-income areas than wealthier ones for groceries.
data.description: In a recent study conducted by the New England Consumer Alliance in Portland, Maine, it was revealed that Hannaford supermarket chain imposes significantly higher prices on identical grocery items in lower-income areas of Maine compared to more affluent neighborhoods. This disparity has raised concerns about pricing practices within the company. The research, published on January 16, 2026, highlights the notable difference in costs for the same basket of goods across various economic regions. The findings underscore potential issues of price discrimination and inequity in access to affordable groceries. The study's outcomes have sparked discussions about the impact of these pricing strategies on communities with fewer financial resources.

data.title: PotlatchDeltic declared 2025 dividend distributions worth $1.80 per share, taxed as 100% Capital Gain Distributions at long-term 20% rate.
data.description: PotlatchDeltic Corporation (Nasdaq: PCH) disclosed the tax treatment for its dividend distributions in 2025 for its Common Stock (CUSIP# 737630103) in Spokane, Wash. The total dividend distributions for the year amounted to $1.80 per share. The company specified that these distributions are classified as 100% Capital Gain Distributions for income tax purposes, falling under the long-term 20% rate bracket. The income tax treatment summary of the company's 2025 dividends is outlined in the following table, which will be reported on Form 1099-DIV.

data.title: Guardian Capital reveals final valuation of GuardBonds 2025 Fund as it reached maturity.
data.description: Guardian Capital LP revealed today the official valuation of the GuardBonds TM 2025 Investment Grade Bond Fund (Cboe: GBFB) upon the Fund's maturity.

data.title: zSpace (NASDAQ: ZSPC) completes strategic restructuring for long-term success, focuses on AR/VR learning solutions.
data.description: On December 11, 2025, zSpace, Inc. shared news of a significant milestone as they concluded a strategic restructuring aimed at improving their cost efficiency and operational effectiveness. The company, renowned for its immersive augmented reality (AR) learning solutions in the education sector, finalized this process to better align with market changes and ensure prolonged success. With a focus on providing cutting-edge augmented and virtual reality (AR/VR) experiences, especially in fields like STEM, CTE, and career readiness programs, zSpace remains devoted to driving educational achievements. Traded on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol ZSPC, the company continues to be a prominent global player in the AR technology realm.

data.title: Mini Mall Storage Properties Trust closes C$750 million debenture offering, largest ever in Canadian real estate.
data.description: Mini Mall Storage Properties Trust successfully closed an offering of C$450 million in Series A senior unsecured debentures and C$300 million in Series B senior unsecured debentures, totaling C$750 million. The debentures mature in 2028 and 2030 respectively and were offered in Canada by RBC Capital Markets and BMO Capital Markets. The Series A debentures have a fixed annual interest rate of 4.284% and the Series B debentures have a fixed rate of 4.751%. The proceeds will be used to refinance debt, acquire properties, and for general corporate purposes. Mini Mall also converted its existing C$525 million revolving credit facility to an unsecured one for greater flexibility. Avenue Living Group, the parent company, has $9 billion in assets under management and focuses on long-term investments impacting North Americans. Risk factors include financing, economic conditions, and interest rates.

data.title: Branded Legacy, Inc. changes leadership with Dr. Jamie Forrest leading takeover after acquiring controlling interest.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on December 10, 2025, Branded Legacy, Inc. (OTC: BLEG) revealed a significant overhaul and shift in control subsequent to shareholder Dr. Jamie Forrest, PhD, MPH acquiring the majority stake. The Company announced this restructuring without specifying further details.
